mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines as the COVID-19 shock disrupts supply chains, reducing real economic output and lowering the real rate of return on capital, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield downward over the next month.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward yield change is positive in more than 50% of trading periods where INDPRO declines month-over-month, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate spikes above the natural rate threshold of 5.5%, investors expect a deep recession and a flight to safety, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to drop sharply within the next week.
failure_condition: If five days after crossing the 5.5% threshold the 10-year yield rises (or falls less than 10 bps) in more than 60% of occurrences, this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero federal funds rate and rapidly rising unemployment signals that conventional monetary policy is exhausted, forcing the Fed to resort to aggressive quantitative easing that compresses long-term yields over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward yield change is positive in more than one-third of periods where both conditions hold, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in retail sales (RSAFS) of more than 2% month-over-month for two consecutive months signals a severe contraction in consumer demand, which depresses inflation expectations and real activity, driving 10-year yields lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines >2% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If after the trigger the 20-day yield change is positive in more than 40% of observations, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP (GDPC1) contracts sharply in Q1 2020 as the pandemic halts economic activity, confirming a severe recession that weakens long-run growth expectations and lowers the equilibrium real rate, permanently depressing the 10-year yield over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward yield change is positive in more than half of the quarters where GDP declines year-over-year, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5%, the central bank can no longer use traditional easing and announces large-scale asset purchases that directly drive down the 10-year Treasury yield over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If after the trigger the 20-day yield change is positive in more than 30% of events, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts (HOUST) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate a synchronized collapse in housing and labor markets, compounding recession fears and pushing investors into safe assets, which lowers the 10-year yield over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and PAYEMS decline month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If after the joint trigger the 60-day yield change is positive in more than 20% of occurrences, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in nonfarm payroll employment signal the onset of a recession that will deepen, compelling the Federal Reserve to maintain an ultra-accommodative stance, which anchors the 10-year yield at low levels for the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield change is positive in more than 40% of instances after the trigger, this hypothesis is rejected.
